examples:
Command Example
!msgraph-groups-list-groups top=4
Context Example
{
"MSGraphGroups": "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/groups?$top=4&$skiptoken={skip_token}"
}
Human Readable Output
Groups (Note that there are more results. Please use the next_link argument to see them.):

!msgraph-groups-list-groups top=4
Context Example
{
"MSGraphGroups": "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/groups?$top=4&$skiptoken={skip_token}"
}
Human Readable Output
Groups (Note that there are more results. Please use the next_link argument to see them.):
ID
name: msgraph-groups-list-groups

outputs.description: A classification for the group (such as low, medium or high business impact). MSGraphGroups.CreatedDateTime The timestamp when the group was created.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroups.Classification

outputs.description: The timestamp when the group was deleted. MSGraphGroups.Description An optional description for the group.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroups.DeletedDateTime

outputs.description: Specifies the group type and its membership. If the group collection contains a Unified value, the group is an Office 365 group; otherwise it's a security group. If the collection includes DynamicMembership, the group has dynamic membership; otherwise, membership is static.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroups.GroupTypes

outputs.description: The unique identifier for the group. MSGraphGroup.IsAssignableToRole Whether the group assigned to a specific role.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroups.ID

outputs.description: The SMTP address for the group. For example, "serviceadmins@contoso.onmicrosoft.com". MSGraphGroup.MailEnabled Specifies whether the group is mail-enabled.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroup.Mail

outputs.description: The mail alias for the group, which is unique in the organization. MSGraphGroup.OnPremisesDomainName Contains the on-premises domain FQDN. Also called dnsDomainName, which is synchronized from the on-premises directory.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroup.MailNickname

outputs.description: Indicates the last time at which the group was synced with the on-premises directory. The Timestamp type represents date and time information using ISO 8601 format in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2019 is '2019-01-01T00:00:00Z'. MSGraphGroup.OnPremisesSyncEnabled Whether this group is synced from an on-premises directory (true). This group was originally synced from an on-premises directory but is no longer synced (false). Null if this object has never been synced from an on-prem
outputs.name: MSGraphGroup.OnPremisesLastSyncDateTime

outputs.description: Email addresses for the group that directs to the same group mailbox. For example: ["SMTP: example@demisto.com", "smtp: example@demisto.com"]. MSGraphGroup.RenewedDateTime Timestamp of when the group was last renewed, which represents the time and date information using ISO 8601 format. Always in UTC time. For example, midnight UTC on Jan 1, 2019 is '2019-01-01T00:00:00Z'.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroup.ProxyAddresses

outputs.description: Specifies whether the group is a security group. MSGraphGroup.Visibility Specifies the visibility of an Office 365 group. Can be: "Private", "Public", or "Hiddenmembership". Blank values are treated as public.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroup.SecurityEnabled

outputs.description: The URL of the next results page.
outputs.name: MSGraphGroup.NextLink
